previousState: revoked (state/linkedin-auth-business.json tokenExpiry 2026-07-11, no refresh token, products OpenID Connect + Share on LinkedIn only, missing Marketing Developer Platform)
currentBlocker: OAuth browser approval — script generated auth URL, callback listener closed, awaiting Ken to click Allow in browser
scopesRationale: Read-only Marketing/Advertising API set per LinkedIn Increasing Access doc (li-lms-2026-06) Advertising API program permissions table. r_ads for ad accounts, r_ads_reporting for reporting, r_basicprofile/r_organization_social/r_organization_admin/r_1st_connections_size for member+org reads required by ad endpoints. Write scopes (rw_ads, w_organization_social, w_member_social) explicitly excluded to keep this a non-mutating probe.
authorizationUrlExpiresAt: 2026-07-15T08:40:00Z (state param valid 30 min from issue; if past, re-run auth script for a fresh URL)
instructionsForKen: 1. Open the authorizationUrl above in any browser (the script also auto-attempted 'open' on macOS).
2. Log in as the AInchors business account (the one approved for Marketing/Advertising API product on app 86fb2cb4ga03jy).
3. Review the consent screen — it will list 9 scopes (3 OpenID Connect + 6 Marketing/Advertising read).
5. Browser will redirect to http://localhost:8765/callback?code=... — local listener MUST be running for the script to capture the code. The listener is started by the script itself.

6. Yoda should re-run: zsh scripts/linkedin-auth.sh --account business  (this will start a new listener on 8765 AND attempt to open the URL again; user can reuse the URL or let the script generate a fresh one).
7. If the script is started in the same workspace session as Ken's browser, the listener will catch the redirect and the script will continue automatically.

postAuthPlan.step1_verify_keychain: Confirm both access and refresh tokens are stored: security find-generic-password -a linkedin -s ainchors-linkedin-business-access-token -w  ;  security find-generic-password -a linkedin -s ainchors-linkedin-business-refresh-token -w
postAuthPlan.step2_update_state: Update state/linkedin-auth-business.json with new authorizedAt, tokenExpiry, refreshTokenPresent, and a liveVerification block recording Marketing/Advertising API products, allowedScopes, and the userinfo HTTP code.
postAuthPlan.step3_expected_outcome: HTTP 200 with JSON body listing ad accounts the authenticated member can administer. An empty elements array is fine (account may have no ad accounts). 401 = token issue. 403 = product/scope not actually approved. 404 = endpoint path issue. 5xx = LinkedIn side.
step4_token_probe_fallback_endpoints: GET https://api.linkedin.com/v2/organizations  (read orgs the member admins)
postAuthPlan.step5_update_token_health: Write state/linkedin-token-health-business.json with the probe result (HTTP code, sanitized body summary, error code if any).
postAuthPlan.step6_close_out: Mark TKT-1001 done. Append CHG-0887 verification entry. Update state/linkedin-auth-business.json liveVerification block.

deliverablesSoFar.businessGuardRemoved: Replaced the hard 'Marketing Developer Platform missing' exit-1 block with a historical note pointing to the backup. Syntax validated with zsh -n.
